englishDefinition: At what time, or at the time that something happens.
In or to what place; at what location.
Used to introduce two or more choices.
Used to ask about a choice between two or more things.
During the time that something else is happening.

Used to ask about the name or identity of a person.
All of something; complete; entire.
Used to ask which person, when that person is the object of an action.

Used to ask the reason for something.
Measuring a long distance from one side to the other.

The distance from one side of something to the other.

Living or growing in nature, not under the care of people.
Ready and happy to do something.
To finish first in a contest or game.
Air that moves naturally over the surface of the earth.

Having a lot of wind.

The body part used by birds and planes to fly.

The coldest season of the year, between autumn and spring.
Having or showing good sense and good judgment from experience.
To want something to happen, especially something unlikely.
Together with another person or thing; using something.
Inside; before a certain time has passed.
Not having something; not in the company of.
A round-bottomed cooking pan used in Asian cooking.
A wild animal like a large dog that often hunts in groups.
An adult female human.
A public toilet for women.
To want to know something, or to feel surprise at something amazing.
Extremely good; making you feel happy.

Made of wood.

The earth and all the people, places, and things on it.

Most bad; the lowest in quality.
Having a particular value, or deserving of effort.
An injury to the body, often caused by a weapon or accident.

To put information on paper so you do not forget it.

Happening once each year.

Up to now; or used to introduce something different.
